name: Embalse de Guajaraz — circular from Argés
name_original: Embalse de Guajaraz — circular desde Argés
region_intro: Hiking around Argés offers routes through the varied landscape of the Toledo province, characterized by its plains, river valleys, and the nearby Embalse de Guajaraz reservoir. The region features a mix of open countryside and areas alongside the Tagus River, providing diverse scenery for outdoor activities. Trails often traverse gentle hills and agricultural lands, with some routes exploring historical areas near Toledo. The terrain is generally accessible, making it suitable for a range of hiking abilities. Best hiking trails around Argés The most popular hiking route is Embalse de Guajaraz — circular from Argés, a 6.6 miles (10.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 49 minutes to complete. This circular path offers views of the reservoir and surrounding rural landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Route of the Three Castles (PR-TO 38), a moderate 8.6 miles (13.8 km) path. This route connects historical points, providing a blend of cultural interest and natural scenery. Local hikers also love the Azucaica from Toledo via the Tajo Natural Path (GR-113) — round trip, a 10.1 miles (16.3 km) trail leading through the Tagus river valley, often completed in about 4 hours 11 minutes. Hiking around Argés is defined by river paths, reservoir views, and open countryside. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate day hikes. The routes in Argés are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 260 reviews. More than 2100 hikers have used komoot to explore Argés's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Argés?
A: Argés offers a wide selection of routes, with over 90 hiking trails available. These range from easy strolls to more challenging day hikes, ensuring options for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking routes suitable for beginners in Argés?
A: Yes, Argés has plenty of options for beginners and those looking for an easier walk. Approximately 42 of the trails are rated as easy, often traversing gentle hills and open countryside. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40364730" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Martín Bridge – Toledo Cathedral loop from El Greco Museum&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 6.6 km route.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes around Argés?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular choice in the area. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018261137/embalse-de-guajaraz-circular-from-arges" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Guajaraz — circular from Argés&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic views of the reservoir and surrounding rural landscape over 10.7 km.

Q: What are the best times of year to go hiking in Argés?
A: The best times to hike in Argés are generally spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, making for comfortable hiking conditions. Summers can be very hot, and winters are typically mild but can be damp.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Argés?
A: Many of the easier trails in Argés are suitable for families. These routes often feature accessible terrain and shorter distances, allowing for an enjoyable outing with children. Look for trails rated as 'easy' on komoot for the most suitable options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Argés?
A: Hiking in Argés typically involves varied terrain, including open countryside, agricultural lands, and paths alongside the Tagus River. You'll encounter gentle hills and some routes exploring historical areas. The landscape is characterized by plains and river valleys, with generally accessible paths.

Q: Are there any historical sites or attractions to see along the trails near Argés?
A: Yes, the region around Argés is rich in history. Many trails offer opportunities to see historical sites. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1019433717/route-of-the-three-castles-pr-to-38" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Three Castles (PR-TO 38)&lt;/a&gt; connects several historical points. Nearby attractions include the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5453856" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Servando Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1035562" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toledo Cathedral&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is it possible to hike with dogs on trails in Argés?
A: Many trails in the Argés area are dog-friendly, especially those in open countryside and natural areas. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, particularly near agricultural land or wildlife, and to check local regulations for specific routes or protected areas.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Argés?
A: The trails in Argés are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 260 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse scenery, from reservoir views to river paths, and the well-maintained network of routes suitable for various abilities.

Q: How can I get to the hiking trails in Argés using public transport?
A: While Argés itself is a smaller town, public transport options, primarily buses, connect it to the larger city of Toledo. From Toledo, you may find local bus services that can take you closer to specific trailheads. It's recommended to check local bus schedules for the most up-to-date information.

Q: Where can I park when hiking in Argés?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Argés, particularly near the town center or at designated starting points for popular trails. For routes like those around the Embalse de Guajaraz, you can often find informal parking areas. Always ensure you park responsibly and do not obstruct access.

Q: Are there any hikes that offer views of the Embalse de Guajaraz?
A: Yes, the Embalse de Guajaraz is a prominent feature in the Argés landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018261137/embalse-de-guajaraz-circular-from-arges" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Guajaraz — circular from Argés&lt;/a&gt; route is specifically designed to showcase the reservoir, offering beautiful views of the water and its surroundings.

Q: What are the typical durations for hikes in Argés?
A: Hikes in Argés vary in duration. Many easy routes can be completed in 1-2 hours, while moderate trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1343866607/azucaica-from-toledo-via-the-tajo-natural-path-gr-113-round-trip" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azucaica from Toledo via the Tajo Natural Path (GR-113) — round trip&lt;/a&gt;, might take around 4 hours. There are options for both short walks and longer day hikes.
tips: Built on the Tagus hydrographic basin and very close to the town of Argés, the reservoir offers a wide variety of routes and trails to explore it in the way you prefer, you can even go around it, although it may be too long to walk.
